news.summary: Federal prosecutors are pursuing a case against Tornado Cash founder Roman Storm but will drop a charge related to operating an unlicensed money transmitting business, aligning with the Department of Justice's recent rollback of crypto enforcement. The indictment will focus on Storm allegedly transmitting funds from criminal activities or to support illegal actions. Prosecutors claim Tornado Cash helped launder over $1 billion in crypto, including for the North Korean group Lazarus. Storm faces charges of money laundering conspiracy and violating US sanctions, each carrying up to 20 years in prison, and the unlicensed money transmitter conspiracy charge carries up to five years. Storm has pleaded not guilty, and his trial is set for July 14. Storm's associate Roman Semenov, believed to be in Russia, is also charged. Other crypto executives have cited the DOJ's shift in enforcement to seek dismissal of their charges, including Samourai Wallet's co-founders and SafeMoon's CEO.
news.headline: Tornado Cash founder's trial continues with minor adjustments due to DOJ policy change. $1 billion crypto laundered. Trial set for July 14.

news.summary: In Q1 2025, credit card lending in Hong Kong decreased by 5.8%, as reported by the Hong Kong Monetary Authority. This decline signifies a notable departure from the growth observed in the previous quarter.
news.headline: HKMA: Hong Kong credit card lending drops 5.8% in Q1 2025, reversing previous growth trend.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Tenaya Therapeutics Inc. (NASDAQ: TNYA) in South San Francisco granted stock options for 461,000 shares of common stock to three new non-executive employees as part of their employment commencement. The exercise price is set at $0.4373 per share, matching the closing price of Tenaya’s common stock on the same day. These stock options have a ten-year term and vest over four years: 1/4 will vest on the one-year anniversary of hire, and 1/48 will vest monthly afterward, contingent upon continued service with Tenaya.
news.headline: Tenaya Therapeutics granted 461,000 stock options worth $0.4373/share to 3 new employees; options vest over 4 years.

news.summary: In a recent event held in Seoul, South Korea on May 16, 2025, LG Electronics (LG) displayed a groundbreaking technology demonstrating satellite-based voice communication from a moving vehicle. This innovation highlights LG's position as a leading technology provider in the mobility sector. The showcase featured the utilization of Internet of Things - IoT solutions in enabling this seamless voice communication. This achievement marks a significant step towards enhancing safe and connected mobility experiences.
news.headline: LG showcases satellite-based connected car solution at 2025 5GAA conference in Seoul. Demonstrates voice communication from a moving vehicle. LG pioneers safe and connected mobility.

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Shanghai-based company ArkBiopharmaceutical Co., Ltd. unveiled encouraging findings from the Phase II trial of their new anti-fibrotic medication AK3280 to tackle idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis (IPF). The study, overseen by Professor Huaping Dai, reported positive top-line results, indicating a significant step forward in the treatment of IPF. This development marks a crucial milestone for ArkBio as they strive to address the needs of patients with this debilitating condition.
news.headline: ArkBio reports positive Phase II results of AK3280 for IPF treatment led by Prof. Huaping Dai, Shanghai.

news.summary: In Saskatoon, Saskatchewan, ZYUS Life Sciences Corporation (TSX-V: ZYUS) has completed the second and final part of its non-brokered private placement. The Canadian life sciences company concentrates on creating and marketing innovative cannabinoid-based pharmaceutical drugs for pain relief. This announcement follows earlier news from May 1 and May 6, 2025. The completion of the Second Tranche marks a significant step in the Offering of units from the Company.
news.headline: ZYUS Life Sciences closes final tranche of unit offering, focused on cannabinoid-based drugs for pain management. TSX-V: ZYUS.

news.summary: Thousands of cyclists flooded the streets of San Francisco on May 15, 2025, to celebrate Bike to Wherever Day. The event saw tens of thousands of Bay Area residents participating in the regionwide celebration. This annual event is part of the Bay Area Bike to Wherever Days initiative, promoting cycling and sustainable transportation options. The exact number of participants was not disclosed, but the turnout was significant, highlighting the community's enthusiasm for biking. The event aimed to encourage more people to bike for commuting and recreation, promoting a healthier and more eco-friendly lifestyle.
news.headline: Bay Area sets record with tens of thousands of cyclists on May 15 Bike to Wherever Day celebration.

news.summary: In a press release dated May 15, 2025, Milwaukee-based Brady Corporation (NYSE: BRC) announced that its Board of Directors approved a dividend for holders of the company's Class A Common Stock. The declared dividend amounts to $0.24 per share and is scheduled to be paid on July 31, 2025. Shareholders recorded as owning the stock by the end of business on July 10, 2025, will be eligible to receive the dividend.
news.headline: Brady Corporation declares $0.24 dividend per share to shareholders of Class A Common Stock, payable on July 31, 2025.

news.summary: Leading small-cap stocks showed resilience as the Russell 2000 index approached a significant level. Carvana displayed strength by breaking out of a cup-shaped consolidation pattern. The latest update on the stock market highlights the S&P 500 index turning positive for the year, contrasting with the struggles faced by the Dow Jones Industrial Average. Furthermore, Robinhood, a popular trading platform, is nearing a new high according to recent reports.
news.headline: S&P 500 is positive for the year, Dow struggles. Robinhood nears a high. Small caps, Russell 2000, Carvana in focus.

news.summary: The ongoing surge in the stock market is close to transitioning into a strong upward trend. This development indicates potential buy opportunities for Walmart and a competitor of Tesla as they approach key price levels. The Dow Jones Futures suggest the market is at the final stage before a highly favorable trend, which is reflected in the promising position of the Tesla rival for investors looking to make strategic purchases.
news.headline: Dow Jones futures show highly bullish trend, with stock market rallying towards power trend. Walmart and Tesla rival near buy points.

news.summary: Exro Technologies Inc., a prominent technology firm focusing on power control solutions for electric vehicles and energy storage, disclosed its financial results for the first quarter ending on March 31, 2025. The company operates under the symbol "EXRO" on the Toronto Stock Exchange (TSX). The announcement was made on May 15, 2025, in Calgary, AB. This update includes vital information for investors and stakeholders interested in the company's performance during the initial months of the year.
news.headline: Exro Technologies (TSX: EXRO) announces Q1 2025 results for power control solutions; financials for quarter ended March 31 disclosed.

news.summary: EUGENE, Oregon - Mark your calendars, Eugene residents! Greenhill Humane Society is organizing the Bark in the Park event at Alton Baker Park on May 18. The event includes a Walk/Run spanning distances of 2K, 5K, and 10K, as well as various activities for dogs, vendor booths, and entertaining demonstrations. Don't miss out on this exciting event!
news.headline: BMW of Eugene partners with Greenhill Humane Society for 31st Annual Bark in the Park with 2K/5K/10K Walk/Run on May 18 at Alton Baker Park in Eugene, OR.

news.summary: Columbus Circle Capital Corp I announced the pricing of their upsized initial public offering of 22,000,000 units at $10.00 per unit. Each unit includes one Class A ordinary share and one-half of a redeemable warrant, with a whole warrant allowing the holder to purchase one share at $11.50. The units are set to be listed on Nasdaq under the symbol "CCCMU" starting May 16, 2025. The underwriters have a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 3,300,000 units. The anticipated closing date for the offering is around May 19, 2025.
news.headline: Columbus Circle Capital Corp I prices $220M IPO of 22M units at $10 each, listed on Nasdaq as "CCCMU" on May 16.

news.summary: A new weekly show named "Ceasefire" will be launched on a popular public affairs channel after a hiatus of two decades. The key concern is whether members of the Republican and Democratic parties will participate in the show.
news.headline: C-SPAN set to launch “Ceasefire,” its first new weekly show in 20 years. Will it promote civil discourse between Democrats and Republicans?

news.summary: On May 15, 2025, Innovent Biologics, Inc., a leading biopharmaceutical company based in Hong Kong (HKEX: 01801), announced its operations in San Francisco and Suzhou, China. The company specializes in developing, manufacturing, and distributing top-notch medications for oncologic, autoimmune, cardiovascular, and other medical conditions.
news.headline: Second Phase 3 study of Mazdutide vs Semaglutide in China completes first participant dosing for adults with overweight or obesity and fatty liver disease.

news.summary: Boehringer Ingelheim Resort, Inc., a pharmaceutical company, is reportedly accused of not properly documenting the total hours worked by its employees. This alleged negligence has led to discrepancies in the payment and official wage records of the employees. The incident took place in ALAMEDA, California on May 15, 2025. The company is facing allegations of providing inaccurate and inadequate wage statements to its workforce.
news.headline: Lawsuit filed against Boehringer Ingelheim Resort, Inc. for unpaid overtime wages and inaccurate wage recording.

news.summary: The State of Wisconsin Investment Board (SWIB) has sold all 6,060,351 shares ($355.6 million) in BlackRock's iShares Bitcoin Trust ETF in Q1, with no Bitcoin ETF positions reported in their 13F filing. SWIB initially bought $164 million worth of Bitcoin ETFs in Q1 2024. The Abu Dhabi sovereign wealth fund Mubadala purchased an additional 491,439 IBIT shares in Q1, bringing their total to 8,726,972 ($512 million). IBIT surpassed $45 billion in net inflows by May 14, with a 20-day streak of inflows ending on May 13. Other Bitcoin ETFs trail IBIT in net inflows, with Fidelity Wise Origin Bitcoin Fund ($11.6 billion) and ARK 21Shares Bitcoin ETF ($2.7 billion) being notable ones.
news.headline: Wisconsin Investment Board liquidates 6.1M IBIT shares, worth $355.6M. SWIB previously held $164M BTC in Q1 2024. Abu Dhabi's Mubadala buys 491.4K IBIT shares worth $512M. IBIT sees $45B net inflows, 20-day streak broken. FBTC, ARK trail IBIT in net inflows.

news.summary: In Golden, Colorado and Montreal, the Molson Coors Beverage Company has announced its quarterly dividend declaration. The specific amount of the dividend was not mentioned in the provided information.
news.headline: Molson Coors announces regular quarterly dividend declaration in Golden, Colo & Montreal via Business Wire.
